SessionStart.name: upgrade-plugin-welcome
SessionStart.description: Announce available upgrade commands on session start

hooks.prompt: The odoo-upgrade plugin (v5.1) is active. Available commands: /odoo-precheck <path> (scan for issues), /odoo-quickfix <path> (safe mechanical fixes), /odoo-upgrade <path> <version> (full migration pipeline). The plugin protects core Odoo files from modification and checks for version compatibility issues in real-time.

PreToolUse.name: core-odoo-guard
PreToolUse.description: Block Write/Edit of core Odoo framework files (odoo/addons/, odoo/*.py)

PostToolUse.name: odoo19-compat-check
PostToolUse.description: Check written files for Odoo version compatibility issues (tree->list, attrs, jsonrpc)

PostToolUse.name: bash-compat-error-detect
PostToolUse.description: Detect Odoo version compatibility issues in Bash command output

hooks.prompt: Check if the output indicates an Odoo version compatibility issue. Look for patterns like: 'Invalid field', 'Service rpc is not available', 'Invalid view type', 'cannot be located in parent view', 'cannot import name slug', 'cannot import name.*url_for', 'Invalid view definition', 'Entity.*not defined', 'context_today.*strftime', 'AttributeError.*tzinfo', 'numbercall', 'kanban-box'. If it is a compatibility issue, respond {"ok": true, "reason": "This looks like an Odoo version compatibility issue. Consider using the odoo-upgrade skill or /odoo-precheck to diagnose and fix it."} otherwise {"ok": false}.
